hypothesis: An active-learning-augmented ML-SSNEB workflow that selectively queries DFT evaluations for images with high surrogate uncertainty and retrains the model online will enable reliable, material-general convergence to DFT-level MEPs across diverse solid-state transitions (e.g., CsPbI3 and TiO2 under varying P–T) with barrier predictions within 5% of full DFT values while achieving at least a 2× reduction in total computational time compared to non-adaptive ML surrogates.
